title: salvia pratensis var. albiflora
intro: Salvia pratensis var. albiflora, also known as white meadow clary, is a rare and unique variety of the common meadow clary plant, characterized by its white, fragrant flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits associated with Salvia pratensis var. albiflora include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome and stomach ulcers, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of Salvia pratensis var. albiflora typically involve infusing the flowers in hot water to create a tea, or using the plant's leaves and flowers in a tincture or salve.
